url.scheme.message: Target only supports HTTP(S) request URLs.

headers.unsafeWhen: Duplicate header names may be coalesced by generated JavaScript header structures.

body.multipart.message: axios supports multipart bodies through FormData.

auth.basic.message: axios can send basic auth through the Authorization header.

cookies.inline.message: axios can send explicit Cookie headers.

cookies.jar.message: axios target does not load curl cookie jar files.

proxy.message: Node Axios supports HTTP(S) proxy objects; SOCKS or proxy TLS/header replay requires custom agent wiring.
proxy.unsafeWhen: SOCKS proxies, proxy TLS settings, and proxy-only headers require a caller-provided proxy agent helper.

tls.verify.message: axios can use HTTPS agents for TLS verification settings.

tls.ca.message: Node Axios can use https.Agent for per-request TLS CA settings.

tls.client-cert.message: Node Axios can use https.Agent for TLS client certificate settings.

network.message: axios renderer does not yet preserve curl network binding or connection routing.

dns.message: axios renderer does not yet preserve curl DNS resolver overrides.

redirects.message: axios supports redirect behavior through Node adapter options.

http.version.2.message: Axios HTTP/2 support is Node HTTP adapter and runtime dependent, and HTTP/2 redirects are limited.
http.version.2.requiredRuntime: Node.js Axios HTTP adapter with HTTP/2 support.

http.version.3.message: axios does not expose HTTP/3 selection.

debug.message: axios output cannot reproduce curl verbose or trace wire dumps.

external-ref.message: Generated code must provide runtime handling for external references.
external-ref.requiredRuntime: Node.js filesystem access to referenced inputs.
